beats.activity: Wake up, coffee by the philodendrons, check on houseplants.
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning ritual: Sage starts every day with coffee among the plants. Low energy yesterday (40/100) suggests a restful, unhurried wake-up at home is perfect.

Beautiful sunny Sunday (72F) is perfect for the farmers market. Sage loves this routine and has established a pattern of visiting markets on nice weather days. Budget 30 min for drive from Wedgwood to Ballard.

beats.activity: Browse farmers market, chat with vendors, pick up seasonal produce and plant cuttings.
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning market time is Sage's preferred way to spend a calm day. The sunny weather and relaxed energy fit his laid-back style perfectly.
Drive back across Seattle from Ballard to Wedgwood; budget 40 min for traffic on a Sunday morning.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat brunch with fresh farmers market ingredients, water houseplants.
beats.reasoning: Sage values quiet Sundays at home. Fresh produce from the market becomes a simple, mindful meal. This is his pace for a low-energy recovery day.

beats.activity: Commute to Gas Works Park for afternoon walk.
beats.reasoning: Sunny 72F Sunday is ideal for outdoor time. Gas Works Park is a natural choice for a landscape architect—close to home, beautiful views, and a place to observe plantings and hardscape in action. Budget 30 min from Wedgwood.

beats.activity: Walk through the park, observe plantings and landscape design, sketch ideas in notebook.
beats.reasoning: As a landscape architect, Sage often finds inspiration in public parks. The sunny weather and calm Sunday vibe make this a perfect time for reflection and observation. No boats/crowds at the park itself—the Lake Union traffic is separate.
Drive back from Wallingford to Wedgwood; budget 30 min.

beats.activity: Paint on the porch, abstract work, moving color around while the light is good.
beats.reasoning: Sage's favorite Sunday afternoon activity. The soft late afternoon light on a sunny day is perfect for painting. This is restorative time that helps him reset for the week ahead.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a simple dinner, tidy up the porch.
beats.reasoning: Quiet Sunday dinner at home. Sage values simplicity and reflection. No social plans tonight—this is a wind-down evening.

beats.activity: Evening leisure—read, listen to music, tend to houseplants, maybe sketch landscape ideas.
beats.reasoning: Sage is a night owl. He lingers with quiet evening activities—reading, tending plants, low-key creative work. This is his natural rhythm, especially after a restful day.
Night owl bedtime (late for him), with a solid 7h 45m sleep. Ends well before 06:00, allowing the bar to visibly span overnight. Agent is already at home for the transition.

diary: Woke up slow this morning, coffee steaming while I checked on the philodendrons—a couple leaves were looking a bit droopy, so I gave them some attention. The farmers market in Ballard was perfect; that vendor with the heirloom tomatoes always saves me the good ones. Grabbed some plant cuttings too, couldn't resist. Made brunch at home with the fresh stuff, then headed over to Gas Works to walk it off. The afternoon light was hitting the park just right, and I spent a good hour sketching some ideas about how the native plantings catch the sun at different angles. Back home, I painted on the porch for a couple hours—abstract stuff, just moving color around while the weather held. There's something about a calm Sunday that lets everything settle.
thought_process: Today felt like the kind of day I needed after a week of meetings and site reviews. The weather being perfect meant I could actually be outside observing spaces rather than just thinking about them. I gravitated toward the farmers market first because it centers me—talking to growers, seeing what's in season—it reminds me why I got into landscape work in the first place. At Gas Works, I was genuinely studying the plantings, not just taking a walk. The sketching there fed directly into the painting later; I think I was processing design ideas through color. The quiet Sunday rhythm kept me reflective rather than productive-focused. I chose leisure deliberately because sometimes the best design thinking happens when you're not forcing it.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
